name: Fermentation (Basic)
tagline: Uses microbes to preserve food, produce alcohol, and enable staple transformations
problem:
Fresh food spoils quickly and can be unsafe. Without refrigeration, civilizations need methods to preserve calories and make nutrients more bioavailable.

Fermentation solves this by using controlled microbial processes to acidify, alcoholize, or otherwise stabilize foods. The challenge is control: contamination can harm or waste the batch.

The remaining problem for Fermentation (Basic) is operational reliability. A reader needs more than a name and a historical note: they need to know what failure this capability prevents, what resources it consumes, what signs show that it is failing, and when it is safer to stop than to improvise. This entry therefore treats fermentation (basic) as a teachable system with inputs, inspection points, records, and limits rather than as a single trick.
overview:
Fermentation uses yeasts and bacteria to convert sugars into acids, alcohols, and gases. Many traditions rely on stable starter cultures and workflow hygiene rather than advanced instruments.

For bootstrap, fermentation is both food technology and microbiology education: it teaches contamination control, temperature management, and process repeatability.

In collapse recovery, fermentation (basic) must be repairable with local materials, teachable to more than one person, and documented well enough that the method survives injury, migration, or leadership change. In a space-settlement context, the same capability must account for closed-loop resources, contamination control, spare-part scarcity, power budgets, and the cost of mistakes in a sealed habitat. The practical minimum is a controlled trial, written checks, safe storage, and review before scale-up.

principles.name: Microbial metabolism
principles.explanation: Microbes convert sugars into products that change taste, safety, and shelf life.

principles.name: pH and preservation
principles.explanation: Acidification inhibits many pathogens and spoilage organisms.

principles.name: Process control
principles.explanation: Temperature, cleanliness, and time determine outcomes.

components.name: Use-case boundary
components.function: Defines what Fermentation (Basic) is meant to accomplish and what it must not be used for
components.madeFrom: Written requirements, hazard notes, local constraints, and responsible operator assignment
components.criticalNote: A vague use case leads to unsafe improvisation.

components.name: Input control point
components.function: Checks materials, tools, information, and environmental conditions before work starts
components.position: At receiving, storage, or setup area
components.madeFrom: Labels, inspection notes, gauges, samples, and quarantine bins
components.criticalNote: Unknown inputs should be isolated until checked.

rawMaterials.name: Food substrates
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Hydroponic crops and stored carbohydrates; manage contamination carefully in closed habitats.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Cleaning, crushing/mashing, and sometimes cooking to gelatinize starches.

rawMaterials.name: Clean water
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Closed-loop water; prioritize for hygiene and safe food handling.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Treat water to avoid introducing contamination.

rawMaterials.name: Salt (when used)
rawMaterials.purpose: Selects for desired microbes in pickling
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Recycling salts from life support; manage corrosion and health constraints.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Keep dry and uncontaminated; measure concentration.

buildSteps.title: Choose product and method
buildSteps.description: Decide acid fermentation, alcohol, or mixed; choose vessel and oxygen exposure accordingly.
buildSteps.warningNote: Wrong method can enable unsafe microbes.

buildSteps.title: Sanitize tools and containers
buildSteps.description: Clean thoroughly; use boiling water or approved sanitizers; avoid recontamination.
buildSteps.warningNote: Dirty equipment causes batch failure or illness.

buildSteps.title: Prepare substrate
buildSteps.description: Crush, mash, or cook as needed; cool to inoculation temperature.
buildSteps.warningNote: Hot substrates can kill starter cultures.

buildSteps.title: Inoculate and control environment
buildSteps.description: Add starter; control temperature; limit oxygen if required; record times.

buildSteps.title: Assess and store safely
buildSteps.description: Use smell/taste/visual checks; discard suspicious batches; store in clean containers.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not consume if mold/toxins are suspected.

history.event: Fermented foods appear across cultures as preservation and nutrition technology.
Beer, wine, and leavened breads scale urban caloric systems.

impact:
Fermentation increases shelf life, can improve safety, and creates tradeable products. It also teaches process control and hygiene, which later supports medicine and industry.

For The Codex, fermentation is a foundational capability for both survival and the long chain toward biotech and pharmaceuticals.

sources.title: McGee — On Food and Cooking (fermentation sections)
